task: T5 - Upgrade /listings into the operational inventory command center

commands.scenario: Baseline characterization - old index/table lacked operational inventory controls and next-action columns
commands.observable: Matches showed the shallow ListingsTable usage, ActivityBlock, and simple ready/off-plan filters only; no operational search/filter/sort controls or next-action routing were present before the T5 edit.

commands.scenario: TypeScript typecheck
commands.observable: No stdout/stderr; command passed.

commands.scenario: Lint
commands.observable: Blocked by unrelated pre-existing errors outside T5 ownership: react/no-unescaped-entities in ConversationDetail.tsx, no-explicit-any in EscalationInbox.tsx, react-hooks/set-state-in-effect in SellerUpload.tsx, and react-hooks/immutability in InspectionAudioInput.tsx. The T5-owned AgentListingsIndex warning from the first lint run was fixed; the rerun reported no T5-owned errors or warnings.

commands.scenario: Required legacy route and legacy style scan
commands.observable: No matches in T5-owned files. Out-of-scope matches remain in FinishedListingFlow.tsx and NewListingFlow.tsx due CSS variable fallback strings containing surface-1/raw token values; those creation-flow files were explicitly outside T5 write ownership.

commands.scenario: Scoped owned-file legacy route and legacy style scan
commands.observable: No stdout; no owned-file matches.

commands.scenario: Scoped canonical link scan
commands.observable: No stdout; no /dashboard/listings links in canonical listing app/components.

commands.scenario: T5 gate blocker remediation - follow_up_buyers next action mapping
commands.observable: Matched only AgentListingsTable.tsx:234 follow_up_buyers; no /dashboard/listings match. The action now routes to /listings/<id> with label Follow up buyers.

commands.scenario: T5 blocker remediation - 1280 visual QA table breakpoint source check
commands.observable: Lines 12 and 59 show the desktop table activates only at 2xl while cards remain below 2xl; line 13 preserves the 1180px table width so it no longer activates in the 1280-with-sidebar viewport.

commands.scenario: T5 blocker remediation - finite listing index field contracts
commands.observable: queries.ts defines finite unions for property_type, status, knowledge_status, logistics_status, and primary_next_action; listingIndexActions.ts consumes AgentListingPrimaryNextAction and switches on primary_next_action.

commands.scenario: T5 blocker remediation - primary_next_action contract verifier
commands.invocation: cd frontend && node -e 'const fs=require("node:fs"); const quote=String.fromCharCode(39); const src=fs.readFileSync("src/components/listings/listingIndexActions.ts","utf8"); const expected="review_knowledge,set_logistics,manage_viewings,review_offers,review_documents,upload_documents,follow_up_buyers,open_listing".split(","); const list=src.slice(src.indexOf("SUPPORTED_INDEX_ACTIONS"),src.indexOf("export interface ListingNextAction")); const missingList=expected.filter((action)=>!list.includes(quote+action+quote)); const missingCase=expected.filter((action)=>!src.includes("case "+quote+action+quote)); if (missingList.length||missingCase.length){console.error(JSON.stringify({missingList,missingCase}));process.exit(1)} console.log("contract ok: "+expected.length+" supported actions and explicit cases")'
commands.observable: contract ok: 8 supported actions and explicit cases

commands.scenario: T5 blocker remediation - table file size after utility split
commands.observable: 224 pure LOC, below the 250 LOC threshold.

commands.scenario: T5 gate blocker remediation - TypeScript typecheck
commands.observable: No stdout/stderr; command passed.

commands.scenario: T5 gate blocker remediation - scoped component lint
commands.observable: No stdout/stderr; command passed.

commands.observable: No stdout/stderr; command passed after recording this remediation.

commands.scenario: T5 gate blocker remediation - evidence JSON parse

commands.scenario: Whitespace and conflict-marker diff check
commands.observable: No stdout/stderr; command passed.

commands.observable: No stdout/stderr; JSON is valid.

commands.scenario: T5 server ownership and cleanup clarification
commands.invocation: lsof -nP -iTCP:3000 -sTCP:LISTEN; lsof -p 25996 | awk '$4=="cwd" {print $1, $2, $4, $9}'; ps -p 25996 -o pid=,ppid=,lstart=,etime=,command=; ps -p 25995 -o pid=,ppid=,lstart=,etime=,command=; lsof -nP -iTCP:3197 -sTCP:LISTEN; stat -f '%Sm %N' .omo/evidence/dalya-listings-workspace-rebrand/task-5-browser-qa/transcript.json .omo/evidence/dalya-listings-workspace-rebrand/task-5-browser-qa/listings-1280.png
commands.observable: Port 3197 has no listener and the QA transcript records the T5-owned staged server at /private/tmp/dalya-final-surface-listings-qa exited after SIGTERM with the Supabase stub closed. Port 3000 remains PID 25996/parent PID 25995, cwd /Users/eric/dalya-ai/frontend, command next dev --hostname 127.0.0.1 --port 3000, started Wed Jun 24 19:29:06/07 2026 before the T5 browser QA artifacts written at Jun 24 23:49:01/02 2026. It was not killed because it matches the pre-existing user-facing app session, not the T5 QA-owned server.

acceptance_checklist.criterion: Dense operational command center rather than legacy marketplace styling
acceptance_checklist.result: Implemented a summary strip, operational controls, dense desktop table, and mobile cards using neutral/slate/sage/copper/brick Tailwind tokens.

acceptance_checklist.criterion: Rows/cards show status, property type/community, price, assigned agent, buyer conversations, missing facts/knowledge, logistics, active viewings, open offers, last activity, and primary next action
acceptance_checklist.result: Implemented in AgentListingsTable.tsx using AgentListingSummary T3 fields.

acceptance_checklist.criterion: Search/filter/sort for status, attention needed, active buyers/offers/viewings, and recent activity
acceptance_checklist.result: Implemented in AgentListingsIndex.tsx and AgentListingsControls.tsx. Search covers title, community, subcommunity, building/project, unit, and listing id.

Primary action maps to /knowledge, /logistics, /offers, /documents, or /listings/<id>. The gate-blocking follow_up_buyers action now maps to /listings/<id> with label Follow up buyers because there is no canonical buyers subroute. Scoped /dashboard/listings scan returned no matches.

acceptance_checklist.criterion: Loading, error, empty, and no-results states
acceptance_checklist.result: Preserved loading/error/empty states and added a composed no-results state for active filters.

acceptance_checklist.criterion: No gold/dark legacy classes or raw legacy gold token in T5-owned files
acceptance_checklist.result: Scoped owned-file scan returned no matches.

known_limitations: npm run lint exits 1 because of unrelated existing repo errors outside the T5 write scope; T5-owned warning was fixed before the final lint run.

Authenticated browser QA completed through a staged local frontend on 127.0.0.1:3197 with mock auth/API and passed at 1280, 768, and 375 widths. The T5-owned QA server and Supabase auth stub were cleaned up. The remaining 127.0.0.1:3000 listener is a pre-existing/user-facing Next dev server in /Users/eric/dalya-ai/frontend and was intentionally left running.

The broad required scan still reports out-of-scope creation-flow files (FinishedListingFlow.tsx, NewListingFlow.tsx) containing surface-1/raw token fallback strings; those files are explicitly excluded from T5 write ownership.

adversarial_notes.dirty_worktree: Before T5 edits, page.tsx and queries.ts were already modified, and AgentListingsIndex.tsx/AgentListingsTable.tsx were already untracked. I preserved those changes and only edited the index/table surface plus one new controls component.
adversarial_notes.gate_blocker_remediation: Independent gate found backend primary_next_action follow_up_buyers counted as attention by AgentListingsIndex but falling through to Open listing in AgentListingsTable. The mapping now lives in listingIndexActions.nextActionForListing with SUPPORTED_INDEX_ACTIONS and explicit switch cases for all 8 known backend action values; no /dashboard/listings route was introduced.
adversarial_notes.stale_state: T3 additive fields were verified directly in frontend/src/lib/queries.ts and app/api/listing_inventory.py before consumption. No cached reports were trusted.
adversarial_notes.misleading_success_output: Lint failure was not treated as pass; exact unrelated blockers are listed. A successful typecheck alone was not treated as visual completion.
adversarial_notes.hung_or_long_commands: T5-owned browser QA used a staged Next server on 127.0.0.1:3197 with cwd /private/tmp/dalya-final-surface-listings-qa; transcript cleanup records SIGTERM, Supabase stub closure, and safe temp workdir removal. Current lsof confirms no 3197 listener. The 127.0.0.1:3000 listener started at Wed Jun 24 19:29:06/07 2026 in /Users/eric/dalya-ai/frontend, hours before the T5 QA artifacts, and was preserved as the pre-existing user-facing dev server.
adversarial_notes.flaky_tests: No new tests were added in this scoped T5 implementation; static commands were deterministic on rerun.
adversarial_notes.malformed_input: No new parsing boundary was added; search/filter values are constrained through typed UI state and parser functions for select values.
adversarial_notes.prompt_injection: No untrusted external text is executed or sent to an LLM in this index UI.
adversarial_notes.cancel_resume: No resumable user workflow or persisted operation was added.
adversarial_notes.repeated_interruptions: No mid-operation mutation requiring recovery semantics was introduced.
